typecheck.command: cd quant-dashboard && python3 -m mypy app/ src/ --ignore-missing-imports
typecheck.detail: Success: no issues found in 23 source files

lint.command: cd quant-dashboard && python3 -m ruff check src/ app/

appliedUpdates.description: Removed invalid -j4 flag from test command (pytest doesn't support it without pytest-xdist plugin, which is not installed). Also removed --fix flag from lint command to prevent auto-modifying files during validation checks.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add exemption for pure refactoring/cleanup tasks from the 'write comprehensive tests' requirement. Lint and typecheck cleanup features that don't change runtime behavior should be able to rely on existing test suite passing rather than requiring new tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature misc-lint-typecheck-cleanup correctly skipped new test writing since it only removed unused variables and fixed type annotations — no behavioral changes. The existing 288 tests passing was sufficient verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add exemption for non-behavioral changes from the manual curl verification step (step 5). When a feature only changes lint/typecheck issues without modifying runtime behavior, server startup and curl testing adds no verification value.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature misc-lint-typecheck-cleanup's worker skipped curl verification since the changes were purely static analysis fixes. This was the correct judgment call but the skill procedure doesn't explicitly allow it.

rejectedObservations.observation: Feature scope was slightly broader than described (also fixed F401 unused import cascading from F841 variable removal)
rejectedObservations.reason: Not actionable — cascading cleanup (removing an import that became unused after removing the variable that used it) is normal and correct behavior. The worker's handoff correctly documented the actual scope.
